# JAX: High performance array computing

## Contents

- [Ecosystem](#ecosystem)

# JAX: High performance array computing[\#](#jax-high-performance-array-computing "Link to this heading")

![](./_static/jax_logo_250px.png)

## High performance array computing

JAX is a Python library for accelerator-oriented array computation and program transformation, designed for high-performance numerical computing and large-scale machine learning.

![](./_static/jax-hero.svg)

Familiar API

JAX provides a familiar NumPy-style API for ease of adoption by researchers and engineers.

Transformations

JAX includes composable function transformations for compilation, batching, automatic differentiation, and parallelization.

Run anywhere

The same code executes on multiple backends, including CPU, GPU, & TPU

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,) Installation

[Installation](installation.html#installation)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,) Getting started

[Getting Started with JAX](beginner_guide.html#beginner-guide)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,) JAX 101

[JAX 101](jax-101.html#jax-101)

If you’re looking to use JAX to train neural networks, check out the [JAX AI Stack](https://docs.jaxstack.ai/en/latest/getting_started.html)!

## Ecosystem[\#](#ecosystem "Link to this heading")

JAX itself is narrowly-scoped and focuses on efficient array operations & program transformations. Built around JAX is an evolving ecosystem of machine learning and numerical computing tools; the following is just a small sample of what is out there:

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,) **Neural networks**

- [Flax](https://flax.readthedocs.io/)

- [Equinox](https://docs.kidger.site/equinox/)

- [Keras](https://keras.io/)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,) **Optimizers & solvers**

- [Optax](https://optax.readthedocs.io/)

- [Optimistix](https://github.com/patrick-kidger/optimistix)

- [Lineax](https://github.com/patrick-kidger/lineax)

- [Diffrax](https://docs.kidger.site/diffrax/)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,) **Data loading**

- [Grain](https://github.com/google/grain)

- [TensorFlow Datasets](https://www.tensorflow.org/datasets)

- [Hugging Face Datasets](https://huggingface.co/docs/datasets/)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,) **Miscellaneous tools**

- [Orbax](https://orbax.readthedocs.io/)

- [Chex](https://chex.readthedocs.io/)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,) **Probabilistic programming**

- [Blackjax](https://blackjax-devs.github.io/blackjax/)

- [Numpyro](https://num.pyro.ai/en/latest/index.html)

- [PyMC](https://www.pymc.io/)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,) **Probabilistic modeling**

- [TensorFlow Probability](https://www.tensorflow.org/probability)

- [Distrax](https://github.com/google-deepmind/distrax)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,) **Physics & simulation**

- [JAX MD](https://jax-md.readthedocs.io/)

- [Brax](https://github.com/google/brax/)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,) **LLMs**

- [MaxText](https://github.com/google/maxtext/)

- [AXLearn](https://github.com/apple/axlearn)

- [Levanter](https://github.com/stanford-crfm/levanter)

- [EasyLM](https://github.com/young-geng/EasyLM)

- [Marin](https://github.com/marin-community/marin)

Many more JAX-based libraries have been developed; the community-run [Awesome JAX](https://github.com/n2cholas/awesome-jax) page maintains an up-to-date list.
